title: PowerShell for Incident Response
overview: Purpose: Identify suspicious processes, malware, or abnormal execution paths.

study.overview: Purpose: Identify suspicious processes, malware, or abnormal execution paths.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

splunkExamples.code:
Get-CimInstance Win32_Process |
  Where-Object { $_.Name -match 'powershell|pwsh' -and $_.CommandLine -ne $null } |
  Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine | Format-Table -Wrap

importantCommands.code: Get-Process | Select-Object Name, Id, CPU, Path | Sort-Object CPU -Descending
Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Select-Object ProcessId,ParentProcessId,CommandLine | Sort-Object ParentProcessId

Get-CimInstance Win32_Process |
  Where-Object { $_.Name -match 'powershell|pwsh' -and $_.CommandLine -ne $null } |
  Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine | Format-Table -Wrap
